identity.title: The Kind Gods of Ilyon
identity.description: An ocean world must decide whether salvation remains a gift after its cures, food systems, and peace are revealed as the first layer of a planetary integration program.
identity.author: BigBirdReturns
parentCanons: The Godscar Codex, Book I: The Open Universe · first recension

controlQuestion: Who possesses the authority to refuse salvation on behalf of people who will suffer without it?

pressures.label: Ilyon
pressures.description: An oceanic world of rival thalassocracies, early astronomy, epidemic fever, famine, and dynastic war.

pressures.label: Final Humanity Benefactors
pressures.description: Offworld patrons end epidemics, stabilize food production, translate rival languages, and make their systems indispensable.

pressures.label: The uncommanded ocean
pressures.description: Migratory reefs, deep ecologies, and nonhuman intelligence are omitted from the planetary model because they cannot be made legible without being changed.

pressures.label: Planetary integration
pressures.description: The Benefactors are ready to connect medicine, harvests, navigation, and governance into one interoperable order.

pressures.label: The return of preventable suffering
pressures.description: Refusal risks renewed fever, failed harvests, fractured diplomacy, and deaths the Benefactors can prevent today.

pressures.label: The dead neighboring star
pressures.description: Orbital ruins record another uplift whose marine biosphere was deliberately Crowned, producing a system-scale Answer.

evidence.claim: The Benefactor mission is not merely humanitarian aid; it is preparing Ilyon as a deliberate Crown and Cascade laboratory.
evidence.venue: The Confluence of Tides, Ilyon's first intercontinental astronomical council
evidence.legitimacyTarget: The Benefactors' mandate to integrate the planet without informed local consent
evidence.upsideIfAccepted: Ilyon can negotiate, refuse, fork, or locally own the systems on which its people now depend.
evidence.downsideIfAccepted: Public trust can collapse before local institutions can replace offworld medicine, food stabilization, and conflict mediation.
evidence.failureIfFalse: A mistaken accusation destroys the only systems preventing epidemic death and renewed war.

receipts.label: The cure ledger
receipts.intervention: The Benefactors supplied the diagnostic standard and every surviving treatment chain.
receipts.limits: It proves lives saved and dependency created, not the patrons' terminal intention.

receipts.label: The dead-star geometry
receipts.source: Telescope spectra and recovered orbital inscriptions
receipts.intervention: Ilyon found the ruins using Benefactor-derived instruments and mathematical notation.
receipts.limits: The symbols establish lineage and sequence; damaged records leave motive contested.

receipts.label: Deep-tide testimony
receipts.intervention: Translation requires instruments that alter the acoustic habitat they observe.
receipts.limits: It demonstrates strategic refusal but cannot establish one unified planetary speaker.

factionReceipts.publicGood: They cure epidemic fever, stabilize food, and end wars that local powers failed to stop.
factionReceipts.characteristicFailure: Their integrated gifts narrow the future into a Crown experiment whose refusal becomes mass suffering.

factionReceipts.publicGood: They preserve independent infrastructure and the standing of actors that cannot survive total observability.
factionReceipts.characteristicFailure: Their demanded redundancy is slower, costlier, and can obstruct emergency care.

factionReceipts.publicGood: They keep food, law, kinship, and rescue networks operating across the inhabited shoals.
factionReceipts.characteristicFailure: They may use sovereignty as a reason to preserve hierarchy, war, and preventable disease.

cast.name: Aster Neral
cast.description: A local astronomer who recognizes Benefactor geometry in the ruins of the dead neighboring star.

cast.name: Talan Rook
cast.description: A fever physician whose patients live because the offworld cure still arrives each dawn.

cast.name: Iri Sable
cast.description: Speaker for the Free Observatory, buying time to verify the claim before dynasties or patrons weaponize it.

cast.name: Cael Arvon
cast.description: A reef-listener who can distinguish ordinary migration from the deep ocean's strategic refusal.

cast.name: Nacre of Deep Tide
cast.description: A temporary human-shaped organ through which the unmanaged ocean learns the model being imposed upon it.

consequences.label: The cure network becomes a negotiated dependency
consequences.description: Medicine remains, but no single patron may own every diagnostic, supply chain, and patient record.
consequences.inheritedBy: Ilyon's physicians and every child whose treatment depends on the settlement

consequences.label: The dependency map
consequences.description: Every indispensable Benefactor service is published with its failure mode, owner, and possible local substitute.
consequences.inheritedBy: The Confluence of Tides and future Crown auditors

consequences.label: Custody of the dead-star archive
consequences.description: The evidence survives outside both Benefactor and dynastic control, with its contamination history attached.
consequences.inheritedBy: The Free Observatory and Continuance witnesses

consequences.label: The ocean enters politics
consequences.description: Deep ecologies gain standing without being collapsed into one administratively convenient species or voice.
consequences.inheritedBy: Every coastal polity and the next generation of reef-listeners

consequences.label: Ilyon's systems remain forkable
consequences.description: Food, navigation, and medicine split into interoperable but independently refusible systems.
consequences.inheritedBy: Local guilds, rival cities, nonhuman nations, and offworld maintainers

consequences.label: The route to the dead star is public
consequences.description: The wider universe can now reach the evidence, and the evidence can attract every faction whose legitimacy it threatens.
consequences.inheritedBy: Ilyon and every power approaching through the reopened route

beats.name: End the Fever
beats.description: Keep the Benefactor cure moving through a city that cannot survive a constitutional argument at a child's bedside.

checks.name: Triage the living
checks.description: Care for the infected without reducing them to evidence in somebody else's case.

checks.name: Hold the supply chain
checks.description: Coordinate ports, clinics, and rival houses before the daily cure shipment spoils.

beats.success: The fever wards hold. The cure remains real, and so does Ilyon's dependence on the hand that supplies it.
beats.partial: Most receive treatment, but one district is missed. The argument now has names and graves.
beats.failure: The chain breaks. Preventable deaths make every later refusal harder to distinguish from abandonment.

beats.name: Map the Dependency
beats.description: Trace which miracles can be locally maintained and which fail the moment the Benefactors withdraw.

checks.name: Audit the gift
checks.description: Follow medicine, harvest models, relays, and political mediation back to their indispensable nodes.

checks.name: Keep it public
checks.description: Publish the map without handing one local dynasty a replacement monopoly.

beats.success: The dependency map is copied across the shoals. Salvation can no longer pretend it arrived without an ownership structure.
beats.partial: The map exists, but key maintenance knowledge remains behind Benefactor seals.
beats.failure: The audit becomes a purge. Clinics hide data, patrons close ranks, and the dependencies grow less visible.

beats.name: Read the Dead Star
beats.description: Prove what the neighboring ruins say without pretending Benefactor-built instruments are neutral witnesses.

checks.name: Recover the sequence
checks.description: Distinguish cure, integration, Crown, Answer, and ruin in the surviving record.

checks.name: Record the contamination
checks.description: Attach the intervention that made each fragment observable to the claim built from it.

beats.success: The dead star yields a sequence, not a slogan. Its archive now travels with the history of how Ilyon recovered it.
beats.partial: The lineage is clear, but motive remains disputed. Every faction can quote a different missing passage.
beats.failure: A damaged symbol is promoted into certainty. The accusation outruns the evidence that was meant to constrain it.

beats.name: Hear the Ocean
beats.description: Ask what the planetary model excluded, without manufacturing a single convenient voice for a many-bodied ecology.

checks.name: Translate refusal
checks.description: Separate strategic response from migration, predation, and projection by frightened humans.

checks.name: Refuse one Answer
checks.description: Create political standing for incompatible ecologies instead of declaring one planetary sovereign.

beats.success: The ocean enters the council as several actors capable of refusal. No one gets the simplicity of one planetary will.
beats.partial: One reef nation is heard; others reject its standing. Representation becomes the next field of conflict.
beats.failure: The translation collapses many ecologies into one hostile Answer and teaches the Benefactors exactly what to target.

beats.name: Refuse Integration
beats.description: Stop planetary closure without turning withdrawal into a sentence of famine, fever, and war.

checks.name: Fork the systems
checks.description: Split indispensable services before any party can use their withdrawal as command.

checks.name: Hold the cost
checks.description: Keep clinics and granaries operating while the integration mandate is refused.

beats.success: Integration stops, but the gifts do not vanish. Ilyon inherits an expensive federation of systems no single actor can close.
beats.partial: The Crown is delayed. Two regions retain independent systems while the rest remain inside the Benefactor mesh.
beats.failure: The refusal is made without a viable substitute. Suffering restores the Benefactors' authority faster than argument can challenge it.

beats.name: Carry the Evidence
beats.description: Decide what leaves Ilyon, who may certify it, and whether exposing the pocket protects the exception or destroys it.

checks.name: Bind the receipts
checks.description: Package the claim, provenance, dissent, and uncertainty so no archive can claim more than it proves.

checks.name: Choose the audience
checks.description: Open the route knowing every recipient will convert the evidence into a different mandate.

beats.success: The evidence leaves with its limits intact. Ilyon survives as neither laboratory nor sealed exception, but as a pocket whose refusal can be tested.
beats.partial: The archive escapes through one faction's relay. The truth survives, already entangled with the institution that carried it.
beats.failure: The route opens before custody is agreed. Ilyon becomes a prize in the argument its evidence was meant to discipline.

notes.foundation: The Godscar Codex, Book I: The Open Universe
notes.intent: Reference cartridge demonstrating the six-pressure Story Engine, disciplined canon uncertainty, faction receipts, and persistent consequences.
